channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Spain dismantles major $4.7M manga piracy platform, arrests four
item.description: The Spanish police have dismantled the largest Spanish-language manga piracy platform, operating since 2014, with millions of monthly users from around the globe. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
item.description: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Inside Caller-as-a-Service Fraud: The Scam Economy Has a Hiring Process
item.description: Fraud operations now operate like call centers, complete with hiring, training, and performance tracking. Flare reveals how cybercriminals manage "Caller-as-a-Service" operations like a professional s...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New npm supply-chain attack self-spreads to steal auth tokens
item.description: A new supply chain attack targeting the Node Package Manager (npm) ecosystem is stealing developer credentials and attempting to spread through packages published from compromised accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Teams to get efficiency mode on PCs with limited resources
item.description: Microsoft is preparing to roll out a new Efficiency Mode for Microsoft Teams for systems with limited CPU and memory resources to improve app responsiveness. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
item.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft traces Universal Print issues to Graph API code change
item.description: Microsoft says that an ongoing Universal Print sharing issue that prevents users from creating some printer shares is due to a Microsoft Graph API code change. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
item.description: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carrie...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft releases emergency patches for critical ASP.NET flaw
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation vulnerability. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers vulnerable to spoofing attacks
item.description: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers exposed online remain unpatched against a spoofing vulnerability that was exploited as a zero-day and is still being abused in ongoing attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
item.description: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managince administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
item.description: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
item.description: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Lotus data wiper used against Venezuelan energy, utility firms
item.description: A previously undocumented data-wiping malware dubbed Lotus was used last year in targeted attacks against energy and utilities organizations in Venezuela. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
item.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research published ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK Flaws Expose Thousands of Lantronix and Silex Serial-to-IP Converters
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
item.description: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based 'Antigravity' Tool
item.description: The prompt-injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
item.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted hi...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
item.description: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. Angelo Martino, 41, of Land O'Lakes, Florida, teamed ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stopping Fraud at Each Stage of the Customer Journey Without Adding Friction
item.description: Fraud prevention and user experience don't have to be a tradeoff. IPQS shows how combining identity, device, and network signals stops fraud without adding friction. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UK probes Telegram, teen chat sites over CSAM sharing concerns
item.description: Ofcom, the United Kingdom's independent communications regulator, has launched an investigation into Telegram based on evidence suggesting it's being used to share child sexual abuse material (CSAM). ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 5 Places where Mature SOCs Keep MTTR Fast and Others Waste Time
item.description: Security teams often present MTTR as an internal KPI. Leadership sees it differently: every hour a threat dwells inside the environment is an hour of potential data exfiltration, service disruption, r...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: NGate Campaign Targets Brazil, Trojanizes HandyPay to Steal NFC Data and PINs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new iteration of an Android malware family called NGate that has been found to abuse a legitimate application called HandyPay instead of NFCGate. "The threa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA flags new SD-WAN flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: ​CISA has given U.S. government agencies four days to secure their systems against another Catalyst SD-WAN Manager vulnerability it flagged as actively exploited in attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese APT Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Circles
item.description: China is spying on India's financial sector, for some reason, and it's not putting much effort into it, judging by some stale TTPs....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
